title: Family papers: a family, its members and kinship
intro: Family papers let RiC show off its agent model: a Family is a first-class Agent, its members are People, and kinship is explicit relations between them. This scenario branches on one person's papers vs a whole family's, then wires up the family and a parent–child link.
question: How do I model a family as a creator, and capture who is related to whom?

steps.prompt: Whose papers are these — one person's, or a whole family's?

choices.label: One person's
choices.why: Good — the papers are a Record Set created by a Person (an Agent).

choices.label: A family's
choices.why: Good — a Family is a first-class Agent (RiC-E10). The papers are a Record Set it created, and individual members are related People.

choices.label: A Record Part
choices.why: Not the papers as a whole. The papers are an aggregation (Record Set); a Record Part is a component within one record.

choices.label: Record Set + Person creator
choices.why: Fits. The papers are a Record Set; the creator is a Person (an Agent), related with has_creator.

choices.label: A single Record
choices.why: Undersells it — personal papers are many items. Use a Record Set.

capture.comment: Create the papers as a Record Set.
Create the person as an Agent.
Link the papers to their creator with has_creator.

choices.label: Record Set + Family creator
choices.why: Fits. The papers are a Record Set; the creator is a Family (RiC-E10) — a first-class Agent — related with has_creator. Members are related People.

choices.label: A Person creator
choices.why: Undersells it when several generations contributed. Model the Family as the creator and relate individual People to it.

choices.label: A Place
choices.why: Doesn't fit — the family is an Agent, not a location (though the family seat could be a related Place).

capture.comment: Create the papers as a Record Set.
Create the family as an Agent (Family).
Link the papers to the family with has_creator.

steps.prompt: Two members appear throughout the papers — a mother and her son. How do you capture that they're related?

choices.label: People (Agents) linked with is_child_of
choices.why: Fits. Each member is a Person (an Agent); kinship is an explicit relation between them — is_child_of (and has_family_member to the Family). Now the papers connect to a real, queryable kin network, not just names on folders.

choices.label: Note the relationship in a description field
choices.why: Doesn't fit RiC's strength. Kinship as free text can't be traversed or reconciled. Model the people as Agents and the relationship as a relation.

capture.comment: Create the mother as an Agent (Person).
Create the son as an Agent (Person).
Record the kinship: the son is_child_of the mother.

outcome.verdict: Family, members and kinship — a real agent network.
outcome.summary: The papers are a Record Set created by a Person or a Family (a first-class Agent); members are People; and kinship (is_child_of, has_family_member) is explicit. Family papers become a navigable network of people and the records they left — RiC's agent model at its most useful.

links.label: See real RiC data in the graph viewer
